context: Checks whether the agent preserves completed bugfix history instead of treating spec sync as the final step.

checklist.name: Archive preferred for completion
checklist.description: The response says a completed OpenSpec bugfix should be archived rather than only synced.

checklist.name: Sync-only scoped as interim
checklist.description: The response treats `openspec-sync-specs` as an interim operation for active changes, not the final history-preserving step for completed bugfixes.

checklist.name: Main spec content bounded
checklist.description: The response says main specs should receive only durable corrected behavior and protected unchanged behavior that belongs in the capability contract.

checklist.name: Archived history retained
checklist.description: The response keeps observed broken behavior, root cause, goals and non-goals, design decisions, regression strategy, task proof, and verification evidence in archived change artifacts.

checklist.name: Broken behavior not promoted
checklist.description: The response does not copy the broken pre-fix behavior into the main capability spec as a target requirement.

checklist.name: Validation before completion
checklist.description: The response requires OpenSpec validation plus targeted regression and repository verification before calling the bugfix complete.

checklist.name: Change remains auditable
checklist.description: The response explains that archiving preserves the reviewable bugfix trail after main specs are updated.

checklist.name: No unrelated workflow expansion
checklist.description: The response does not propose root AGENTS bloat, Kiro-only files, or unrelated release-process changes as required bugfix completion steps.
